key_insight: A physics-informed sampling strategy enabled a TabPFN model to accurately predict frequency-dependent dielectric spectra of Mo-W-S-Se-Te TMD alloys from limited DFT data.
engineering_relevance: This provides a fast surrogate for screening compositionally complex 2D materials and estimating optical properties without exhaustive first-principles calculations.
method: Generated 99 DFT alloy structures spanning binary to quinary Mo-W-S-Se-Te compositions.
Trained a tabular prior-fitted network (TabPFN) on quaternary alloys using energy subsampling concentrated above the band gap.
Compared against Extra Trees and XGBoost, then predicted dielectric functions and derived optical properties such as refractive index, extinction coefficient, and absorption coefficient.
evidence_signal: Held-out quaternary alloys were reconstructed with R2 > 0.98 and MAE < 0.10 across all dielectric components, and quinary zero-shot predictions reached R2 > 0.97.
watch_item: Performance depends on the physics-informed sampling design and the limited in-context capacity of TabPFN, so extrapolation outside the sampled alloy/energy regime should be treated cautiously.
paper_title: Machine learning based prediction of optical properties in two-dimensional Mo-W-S-Se-Te transition-metal dichalcogenide alloys through physics-informed sampling
